observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office identify each component of the road network system (road lot, road pavement, drainage and slope protection structures, and other miscellaneous structures) and furnish the Municipal Accountant of the result for proper recording.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following adjusting entry:

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the financial transactions using the appropriate accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009 and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries as proposed below.
To correct the erroneous entry in the recording of the cash advance for Confidential fund

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Reconcile the balances of these accounts and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the abnormal balances; and
Provide disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements as to the details of the abnormal balances and the history thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed, to record the transactions using the appropriate accounts pursuant to the descriptions provided for in COA Circular No. 2015-009.
The Municipal Budget Officer will also establish a subaccount for MOOE in the budget to be used as basis for the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a thorough review of the Due to GSIS account and ensure the prompt remittance of the premiums and loan repayments in arrears amounting to ₱4,018,895.27.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Reconcile the recorded mandatory contributions withheld and loan amortization payments against the actual remittances to identify the breakdown of the unremitted balance per employee and cause the immediate remittance to the HDMF once reconciled or identified; and
Conduct monthly reconciliation of the Due to Pag-IBIG account to prevent unnecessary accumulation of unremitted balances.

We recommended and Management agreed, through the Municipal Accountant to:
Reconcile the recorded taxes withheld against the actual remittances to identify the breakdown of the unremitted balance and cause the remittance to the BIR once reconciled or identified; and
Conduct monthly reconciliation of the Due to BIR account to prevent undue accumulation of unremitted balances.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials:
Locate the duly acknowledged received copies of the transmittal of liquidation documents by the ICFAU and to record its liquidation thereafter, for unliquidated cash advances granted in CY 2015 to 2016 totaling ₱1,950,000.00; and
Obtain copies of duly received transmittal of liquidation documents and make a consistent representation with the ICFAU pertaining to the status of the liquidation documents and the subsequent issuance of its Credit Advice, for unliquidated cash advances granted in CYs 2008 to 2014 totaling ₱8,000,000.00.

We recommended and the General Services Officer agreed to make an updated inventory of the unserviceable properties to include properties other than motor vehicles and heavy equipment and coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office in initiating their immediate disposal in accordance with the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties.
We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to refrain from derecognizing properties that are still existing and awaiting its disposal and submit a schedule containing the details of the properties already derecognized including the reason for the absence of the unserviceable Mini ambulance in the books.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Mayor and other concerned departments, provide explanations/ justification for the non-inclusion of the PPAs charged to 20% DF-Continuing Appropriations in the SAOB.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant agreed to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in recording all purchases of supplies and materials for stocking as required in the NGAS Manual to accurately present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements as at a given period.

We recommended and Management agreed to consider constituting the Inventory Committee in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 to spearhead the conduct of physical count of all types of inventories owned by the Municipality and prepare the duly certified RPCI for submission to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended and the Management agreed:
Through the Municipality’s Inventory Committee, to conduct the annual physical count for all PPEs, prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Audit Team for evaluation not later than January 31 of each year;
Through the Municipal General Services Officer, to prepare separate property cards on all classes of PPE as prescribed in the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Through the Municipal Accountant, to maintain separate ledger cards of all PPE accounts.

We recommended and Management, through the Sanggunian Bayan, agreed to expedite the updating and revision of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 7160 to help meet the financial needs of the Municipality.
We also recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and all other concerned department heads agreed to ensure that the revised rate of fees and charges are just and reasonable taking into consideration the application of LFC Toolkit pursuant to DBM-DOF JMC No. 2019-01.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed, through the Municipal Accountant, to prepare and timely submit the requested subsidiary ledgers to the Audit Team for evaluation.
We also recommended and Management agreed to consider availing the ‘e-NGAS’ accounting system developed by COA by allocating enough budget for its implementation like the database server.

We recommended that Management, through the HRMO, initiate steps in the creation of required positions of the LDRRMO in accordance with Civil Service rules to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.
We further recommended and Management, through the Municipal Budget Officer, agreed to submit a computation showing that indeed the Municipality has not exceeded the PS limitation to justify the elevation of the organization and staffing of the LDRRMO.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit justification on the failure to use the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01, as amended under MC 2016-01.
We further recommended and Management agreed to submit the required documents of GAD AR for further evaluation by the Audit team.

We recommended that Management through:
The Municipal Accountant submit the Comparative Notes to Financial Statements to the Audit Team for evaluation; and
The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, prepare and submit the CY 2024 SCBAA.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to initiate the write-off of the subject dormant accounts by observing the guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 2016-005, as amended by COA Circular 2023-008.
It is also enjoined and Management, through the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when due and demandable and that cash advances and fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose pursuant to Item 6 of the same circular.

We recommended and Management, through the municipal officials/employees concerned, agreed to:
Prepare the Agency Plans identifying the programs, and projects intended to address the concerns of the Senior Citizens and Persons with disability of the municipality for the year;
Submit the respective Accomplishment Reports to the NCSC (for older person) and NCDA (for persons with disabilities).

observations.recommendation: Management, henceforth, be guided by the prices of Agri-Basic Commodities set by DA during this COVID-19 Health Crisis to ensure that the government achieves the best value for money in sourcing essential medical goods.  We further recommended Management to always ensure that disbursements out of government funds should always be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU, through the Municipal Agriculture Office, has exerted all efforts to obtain the needed product at the price most advantageous to the government and within the price ceiling set by the DA.  However, after conducting thorough inquiries in the local markets including in the neighboring LGUs, it was found out that the supply was unavailable and if there is available supply, the price of the item is higher than the recommended price due to the law of supply and demand.  

Thus, the LGU was left with no choice but to procure the item at a price higher than the price ceiling in order to provide fresh vegetables for its constituency as part of providing nutritious food subsidy during the ECQ period.

A Certification issued by the Municipal Agriculture Officer was submitted which certified that the supply of vegetables was unavailable at a cheaper price and there was no other choice but to purchase the available vegetable at the existing price market during the specific time.
observations.reason:
The Management had not submitted supporting documents corresponding to the existing price data and/or preliminary market scanning showing the prevailing market prices and practice during that time that would explain the purchase of these goods at prices higher than the rates specified by the DOH.

The submitted Certification of the Municipal Agriculturist would not suffice unless supported with existing price data and/or preliminary market scanning.

observations.recommendation: Management, henceforth,  be guided by the prices of Essential Emergency Medicines and Medical Devices set by DOH during this COVID-19 Health Crisis to ensure that the government achieves the best value for money in sourcing essential medical goods.  We further recommended Management to always ensure that disbursements out of government funds should always be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction:
At the time of ECQ, the goods and supplies being questioned were of vital importance and time is of the essence.

Most medical supplies were out of stock.  If there are stocks available, the prices of some items were more than the price ceiling set by the DOH.  The LGU was left with no choice but to purchase the items available with the cheapest price in the market at that time.
observations.reason: The Management had not submitted supporting documents corresponding to the existing price data and/or preliminary market scanning showing prevailing market prices and practice during that time that would explain the purchase of these goods at prices higher than the rates specified by the DOH.

observations.recommendation: Seek clearance or certification from National Housing Authority (NHA) on its legal basis on the method of procurement for this type of project.  We further recommended management to strictly observe the provisions in R.A. 10752 for those projects requiring acquisition of real properties.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor explained that initially these kinds of projects were implemented by the NHA.  However, upon the President’s order, the NHA seeks assistance from the LGUs to maximize its implementation.  Nonetheless, the Terms of Reference as well as the contents and manner of bidding were practically provided by the NHA.  It was part of the set-up to have the lots be included in the contract.  By this, it is a package deal to be provided from the participating bidders.  As a requirement, the participating bidders need to have an agreement with lot owners of their intent to sell upon award of contract.  It was further emphasized that the inclusion of lot acquisition was already part of the Memorandum of Agreement in which it has to be bid out as a house and lot package.  Same arrangement was used during the Phase I project implementation by the NHA.

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator emphasized that this is purely an NHA project and the LGU is only a recipient of such project.  The Municipal Accountant raised the same issue with the NHA Regional Office to which they were advised that this arrangement was made possible as such is an emergency in nature.  The LCE emphasized that considering this is a Trust Fund; the Terms of Reference has to be followed to which the LGU only acts as what was required by the agreement.
observations.reason: The requested certification or clearance from NHA was not yet secured by the management.

We recommended that Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately trace the source of the discrepancy of ₱288,906.35 and make the necessary adjustments in the books to reconcile with the confirmed bank balance as of December 31, 2021.

We recommended that Management investigate the whereabouts of these supplies and materials as to why these were not yet delivered to its target beneficiaries, take appropriate actions and inform the Audit Team of subsequent developments. We recommended that Management investigate the delayed release of supplies and materials on various programs to its intended beneficiaries and direct those officials/employees involved to cause the submission of justification.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the lacking Sangguniang Bayan authorization, if any, to support the corresponding disbursements of the above-cited projects;
Prohibit the implementation of projects described in generic or lump-sum appropriations without prior authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan; and
Require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the projects under the 20% Development Fund, henceforth.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Audit Team the documentary requirements for the inspection of the above-cited infrastructure projects undertaken by administration and on similar projects thereafter.
We recommended that Management initiate the necessary steps to repair or make corrective actions on the noted defects in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management review the implementation of these projects and compute for liquidated damages, if necessary. We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to effectively monitor the timelines on the completion of projects especially those projects undertaken by administration so as to provide the needed basic services to its constituents in accordance with Sections 476 and 477 R.A. 7160.

We recommended that the MDRRMO, henceforth, formulate and implement its comprehensive LDRRMP in accordance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 and conduct judicious planning and scheduling to ensure implementation of its disaster prevention PPAs.  We further recommended that Management release and use the LDRRMF only for those identified PPAs in the LDRRMP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the LDRRM Officer incorporate in the LDRMMFIP the projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRM fund of the previous years, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and the related subsequent issuances particularly on the propriety of charging of expenditures to the LDRRMF.  We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to diligently check the purpose of disbursements to ensure correctness in charging of expenses in relation to funding source.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking documentary requirements for evaluation by the Audit Team. Further, we recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of the pertinent provisions stated in P.D. No. 1445, the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and DBM NBC No. 563 to avoid the incurrence of unconscionable or excessive expenditures which are disallowable in audit.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents required to support the validity and legality of the hazard pay to avoid disallowance in audit. We also recommended that Management henceforth comply with the provisions of DBM-DSWD JC No. 1, s. 2018 in the payment of hazard pay and/or other compensation-related Magna Carta benefits.

We recommended that Management procure the common-use supplies and equipment from the Cebu Regional Depot of PS-DBM in accordance with DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011, AO No. 17 and pertinent provisions of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. 9184. We also recommended that Management comply with the documentary requirements stated in GPPB Resolution No. 17-2020 for those CSE items which are not available in the PS-DBM at the time of procurement before pursuing the procurement from other sources.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan to pass an ordinance institutionalizing the release of financial assistance to indigents putting in mechanism such as qualification of indigents, amount of assistance vis-à-vis kinds of disease/diagnosis, reportorial requirements and the like.

Adopt measures in ensuring that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor explained that initially these kinds of projects were implemented by the NHA.  However, upon the President’s order, the NHA seeks assistance from the LGUs to maximize its implementation.  Nonetheless, the Terms of Reference as well as the contents and manner of bidding were practically provided by the NHA.  It was part of the set-up to have the lots be included in the contract.  By this, it is a package deal to be provided from the participating bidders.  As a requirement, the participating bidders need to have an agreement with lot owners of their intent to sell upon award of contract.  It was further emphasized that the inclusion of lot acquisition was already part of the Memorandum of Agreement in which it has to be bid out as a house and lot package.  Same arrangement was used during the Phase I project implementation by the NHA.

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator emphasized that this is purely an NHA project and the LGU is only a recipient of such project.  The Municipal Accountant raised the same issue with the NHA Regional Office to which they were advised that this arrangement was made possible as such is an emergency in nature.  The LCE emphasized that considering this is a Trust Fund; the Terms of Reference has to be followed to which the LGU only acts as what was required by the agreement.
observations.reason: The requested certification or clearance from NHA was not yet secured by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to prioritize the completion of reconstruction and reconciliation of their records on PPE to validate the account balance and the inventory committee to conduct a physical count and submit a report thereon for reconciliation with accounting and property records.

We recommended management to compel the concerned accountable officers and immediately submit liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant and refund unexpended balances.  Otherwise, we recommended management to cause the withholding of their salaries until all advances are fully liquidated.  Henceforth, we advise management to strictly comply with laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the grant and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that management immediately implement the projects / programs / activities funded by the National Government and other agencies in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the implementation of those PPAs.
Also, the Municipal Accountant should be directed to initiate timely liquidation and return the unexpended balances to the source agency after the completion of the intended projects/programs/activities in compliance of the aforecited regulation.

We recommended that the management immediately implement the priority projects in order not to deprive the constituents of the benefits due them and facilitate liquidation thereof.  Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert efforts in identifying and documenting the details of the fund balances to facilitate settlement by the barangay with the source agency so as to fairly present the balance of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that management stop the practice of charging expenditure items that do not partake the nature of capital expenditures and strictly comply with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, of which an updated guidelines was issued through DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in order to maximize the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended management to closely monitor the implementation of all 20% development projects to ensure its timely completion. We require the management to require the department officials concerned to provide explanation as to the delayed/non-implementation of the above projects.  We further advise that the Municipal Planning & Development Coordinator effectively carry out its functions in order avoid incurrence of further delays in the completion of projects.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Engineer to provide justification on the delayed completion of the above-mentioned projects and implement an efficient monitoring system for all on-going projects.  Moreover, we advise the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages when necessary.

In order to avoid sanctions that may be imposed under existing laws, we recommended that the management ensure the early preparation of the Annual Budget with complete attachments and submit the same for review by the SP within three (3) days after approval.

We recommended that management fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that management submit henceforth, copies of all perfected government contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents for the procurement of goods & services within five (5) working days from execution/issuance pursuant with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely auditorial and technical review thereof.

We recommended that management require the BAC to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity (HOPE) and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Section 12.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers and records of accounts duly supported with complete documents to substantiate balances in the financial statement.

Municipal Mayor to execute separate Job Order Contracts to all current Job Order workers charged to projects within the municipality.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the approved DTRs, accomplishment report of all Job Order workers for CY 2017 and henceforth comply with the complete documentary requirements per COA Circular No. 2012-001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor execute separate Job Order Contracts to
all current Job Order workers charged to projects within the municipality.
We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
submit the approved DTRs, accomplishment report of all Job Order workers for CY
2017 and henceforth comply with the complete documentary requirements per COA
Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend that the Municipality of Medellin submit authority for the release of
the funds drawn from the SEF to conduct such project to avoid/prevent disallowance
in audit.

We recommend that the Municipality of Medellin fully implement child protection
programs by strengthening the functionality of the Municipal Council for the
Protection of Children (MCPC) using the functionality indicators stated in DILG
Memorandum No. 2008-126, taking into emphasis the following:
a. Organization of the LCPC through a sangguanian resolution/ordinance or
executive/administrative order by the Local Chief Executive;
b. Conduct of regular quarterly and special meetings, if necessary, supported
with Minutes of Meetings;
c. Integration of PPAs of the LCPC in the Annual Budget, preparation and
implementation of the Annual Work and Financial Plan (AWFP) included in the
LDIP and AIP of the municipality, and preparation of resolution of policies by
the MCPC with further approval of the Sanggunian; and
d. Preparation and submission of accomplishments, included PPAs undertaken based
on the AWFP, State of Children’s Report, and accomplishments reflected in the
LGU Annual Report and in the SOLA.

We recommend that the Municipality of Medellin henceforth create their Local
School Board pursuant to Section 98(b)(3) of Republic Act No. 7160.
We also recommend that the Municipality of Medellin prepare a three-year program
for the strategic allocation of the Special Education Fund and submit the Local Board
Resolutions for CY 2017, School Improvement Plans, and Division Education
Development Plan for auditorial review.

We recommend that the Municipality of Medellin require the consultants to pay to
the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) the amount of deficiency withholding tax and
then submit proofs of payment to the auditor; otherwise, it shall be disallowed in
audit.
We also recommend that municipality henceforth comply with the withholding
tax rate of ten percent (10%) for management and technical consultants; otherwise,
penalties and interests shall be personally liable to those responsible for withholding
and remittance as stated in Section 247(b) of the 1997 NIRC.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to procure consultancy services pursuant to the provisions in Republic Act
No. 9184.
We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensure sufficient and
complete documentation of consultancy payments pursuant to COA Circular No.
2012-001.

We recommend all items in the Annual and/or Supplemental Budgets of the
municipality, especially for items under the 20% Development Fund, be specific in
nature or, if in lumpsum, all projects to be charged therein be through specific
Sanggunian approval.
We also recommend that henceforth no administrative expenditures be appropriated
and/or funded out of the 20% Development Fund and that all projects to be funded
and/or expended out of the Fund partake the nature of investment or capital
expenditures pursuant to the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommend that the Municipality of Medellin submit authority to disburse
payment of the loan, including interests incurred, and henceforth sufficiently
appropriate in the Annual and/or Supplemental Budgets the scheduled loan
payments.
We also recommend that the Municipality of Medellin submit the lacking
documentary requirements for the payments of the loan for auditorial review and
henceforth attach sufficient and complete documentary requirements for payment of
financial expenses, including principal loan payments, pursuant to COA Circular No.
2012-001.
Lastly, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the ledger and
account balance difference of the Loans Payable—Domestic account and make the
necessary adjusting entry.

Based on the following audit observations, we recommend the following:
a) The Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer in enforcing more
efficient and effective administration and collection of real property and special
education taxes so as to lessen the tax receivable account balances;
b) The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the differences between the tax
receivable accounts against their corresponding deferred tax accounts;
c) The Municipal Accountant to give a justification as to the particulars of the
unrecorded receipt and adjust accordingly the Special Education Tax account; and
d) The Municipal Treasurer to ensure that total collections for each tax be equal.

We recommend that the Municipality of Medellin, through the approval of the
Sanggunian Bayan, impose the collection of solid waste management fees, at
appropriate rates, throughout the municipality and appropriate the new fund source
through a supplemental budget for the implementation of the solid waste
management plan.
We also recommend that fines and penalties be also imposed throughout the
municipality for prohibited acts, also at appropriate rates, as laid down in Section 48
of Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Solid
Waste Board, ensure the linkage between the Ecological Solid Waste Management
Plan with the Annual Budget and ensure the implementation of planned programs,
projects and activities in compliance with Republic Act No. 9003.

We recommend that the hiring of job order workers be limited to emergency or
intermittent work and shall not, in any case, be made to perform functions which are
part of the job description of the agency’s existing regular employees.
We also recommend that CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 be strictly
followed especially the transitory provisions otherwise the sanction provided therein
shall be meted.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor administer settlement of currently
unsettled suspensions and disallowances issued to the persons liable and
henceforth exercise due diligence to put into force his/her responsibilities as the
Agency Head in the settlement of the accounts of the municipality pursuant to the
provisions of COA Circular 2009-006.
We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Trace the particulars of the amount of the receivable and reconcile the
account as recorded in the books and henceforth coordinate with the Municipal
Mayor with regard to immediate settlement; and
b. Enforce his/her responsibilities as the Agency Accountant in the settlement of
accounts of the municipality pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.
2009-006.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit
immediately the long outstanding dues to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) and
henceforth properly observe payment of the monthly dues to BIR within the
deadlines set by the agency to avoid incurrence of interests and penalties.
We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant properly resolve and
reconcile differences in the ledger and Statement of Financial Position figures of the
Due to BIR account and henceforth observe proper accounting, monitoring and
reconciliation of the account.

We recommend that management henceforth instruct their end-users or
implementing units to prepare their respective Project Procurement
Management Plans (PPMPs) for their different programs, activities and projects
(PAPs) in the format prescribed by the Government Procurement Policy Board
(GPPB) and use the PPMPs for consolidation into an Annual Procurement Plan
(APP) and linkage to the approved annual budget.
We also recommend that the BAC Secretariat prepare their APP in the format
prescribed by GPPB Circular 08-2015.

We recommend that the Head of Procuring Entity refrain from directly
rewarding a contract to a supplier when under negotiated procurement and to
follow the provisions related thereto in the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of RA 9184.
We also recommend that the BAC Secretariat submit the lacking documents
relating to the above transactions and to ensure complete submission of the
required documents in all procurement activities.
Lastly, we recommend that the procuring entity provide a written approval from the
DBM Secretary with regards to the acquisition and purchase of motor vehicles and
submit a written report to the DILG incorporating the particulars of the purchased
vehicles within 30 days from the completion of the procurement process as mandated
in the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155.

We recommend that management demand the return of the honoraria paid
totaling P20,000.00 from the persons concerned as these cash payments are considered
illegal.
We also recommend that management henceforth comply with the provisions
stated in DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A on the Guidelines on the Grant of
Honoraria to Government Personnel Involved in Government Procurement.

We recommend that management submit the required documents for the above
procurements and henceforth submit the required appropriate and complete
documentation on all procurements especially for Agency-to-Agency
procurements.

We recommend that the amount of P41,061,000.00 representing the claim of the
recipients not listed in the Master List but were allowed to receive be duly
accounted for and submit justification for the release of the cash assistance to
10,605 beneficiaries despite the non-approval by the DSWD Office of the request for
the deviation from the guidelines otherwise, this will be disallowed in audit.
We also recommend that the P2,000.00 (P30,000.00 less P28,000.00) be duly
distributed to ESA totally damaged beneficiaries.
We further recommend that an explanation be made to the nonpayment to the 1,491
beneficiaries totaling P31,010.000.00.

observations.recommendation: We require that the Municipal Government require the immediate liquidation of the said cash advances through demand letters to be signed by the Municipal Mayor or his representative. We also recommend that in case of refusal by the employees to liquidate or settle their accountabilities, direct the withholding of salaries to be applied in satisfaction of the unsettled cash advance pursuant to Section 37 of Presidential Decree No. 1445. Lastly, we recommend that clearances for retiring, resigning or transferring officials be deferred until such time that they have liquidated their outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 No action taken. Verification of the Aging Schedule shows that Cash Advances of more than 1 year had remained unliquidated. Also, some officials/employees were granted cash advances even though their previous advances were not yet liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the travel allowance amounting to P3,200.00 should be refunded and that management should strictly follow Section 15 of Rule XIII, MC 40 s. 1998 to avoid irregular expenditures.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 No refund was issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should adhere strictly to COA Circular 97-002 and Section 93 of PD 1445 requiring payments for government transactions to be made in check and payable directly to the creditor or a cash advance shall be granted to the disbursing officer for a specific purpose. Stop the practice of disbursing government funds thru reimbursement to officials without action. Only Disbursing Officers are authorized to pay in cash through the cash advance issued to them.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 No action taken. Review of the cash disbursement journal shows that there are still expenses made on reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should submit an approved SB Resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into contract for the projects/programs to be implemented under the lump sum appropriation in the Fund. It is the responsibility of the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the 20% Development Fund be utilized for the priority development projects and programs of the Municipality as mandated under DILG & DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1. We also recommend that all projects to be funded out of the Fund should contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 No action taken. Lumpsum appropriation is still present in CY 2016 Annual Budget for the 20% Development Fund. This was reiterated in AOM 2017-001 (16).

observations.recommendation: We recommend the LDRRM Officer religiously submit to the COA auditor copy furnished the OCD and DILG-LGOO the annual LDRRMFIP and monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 No action taken. No annual or monthly report related to LDRRM and/or utilization of LDRRMF was submitted to the COA Auditor and Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management submit basis and explanation for the disbursement of P1,300,000.00 to justify the expenses otherwise these will be disallowed in audit. We also recommend that DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 for the guidelines on the entitlement, release, use and reporting of the confidential fund be strictly followed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 No action taken. Cash advances for the Confidential Fund for CY 2015 remained unliquidated as per schedule of unliquidated cash advances duly submitted to the COA Auditors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the recording of the receivables of the economic enterprises (water and housing) in the books of accounts as required of the NGAS Manual. Collections made should not only be recorded as income but also credited in the receivable account to reflect the correct balance of the account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 No action taken. Collections made were not properly recorded as receivables as required by the NGAS Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer to complete the physical count of PPE and submit a copy of its report to the Auditor. We also recommend to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records in accordance with the regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The GSO Office still failed to complete the physical count of PPE and submit a copy of its report to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the amount of P43,500.00 be refunded by the persons who received the honoraria and the Local Finance Committee and the Local Council for the Protection of Children honoraria in 2014 be refunded. It is also recommended that DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 should be used as a guide in the formulation of Guidelines on the Grant of Honoraria Due to Assignment of Special Projects to prevent irregular disbursements in the future.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The subject honoraria had remained unrefunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the honoraria paid to the medical doctors, job order/contractual employees and the other personnel be refunded for the non stipulation in the contract. We also recommend that the Rn Heals employees submit a copy of their contract with the DOH or other issuances by the department authorizing the payment to justify the claim of the PFPR Honoraria. We further recommend that the other non-health officers/personnel be required to refund their claim of the PFPR Honoraria since PhilHealth Circular No. 10s of 2012 provides that the 20% shall be exclusively utilized as honoraria of the staff of the health facility.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The honoraria paid had remained unrefunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal officials prioritize expenditures on projects and programs that directly support the Philippine Development Plan, the Medium-Term Public Investment Program and the Annual Investment Program and the municipal mayor should ensure optimal utilization of the 20% development fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Management still utilized the 20% not for Developmental Projects but for MOOE and other lump-sum appropriations. The municipal officials concerned should prioritize expenditures on projects and programs that directly support development plans.

observations.recommendation: We recommend all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the amount in excess of P5,000.00 of PEI received, otherwise all those who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable. Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of EO No. 80 as implemented by Budget Circular No. 2014-3 and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all concerned officials and department heads since PEI was also claimed in all officials and employees who received the maximum amount will still have to refund the amount in excess. Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of EO No. 80 as implemented by Budget Circular No. 2013-3 and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 During the exit conference, the department heads commented that the amount will still have to be refunded and that the amount in excess will still have to be refunded by all officials and employees who received the excess.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that municipal officials and employees shall refund the CNA signing bonus they received totaling P720,000 since the grant thereof did not conform to the guidelines provided in DBM Budget Circular No. 2006-1. Henceforth, stop the granting of CNA signing bonus.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2009 Management had not started refunding and deducting from the salary.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the municipal accountant should record regular purchases of supplies through an inventory account as required under Section 114 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. Management should consider providing a stockroom for supplies and materials and issue or distribute them in accordance with the provisions under Chapter 7 of the NGAS Manual. Agency personnel in charge of the inventories should submit a monthly report of supplies and materials used/issued to the municipal accountant for the latter to make the necessary journal entry to take up the expense to present a reliable and accurate inventory and expenses account balances in the municipality’s books.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2009 No action taken. Supplies purchased were not recorded as inventory but were immediately recorded as expense.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should prepare a journal entry voucher to correct the entries made for loans received from the President’s Social Fund using the entries presented in the Annual Audit Report.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2006 No action taken. Adjustments to the books were still not made.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate the amount enough to pay the loans and the interest payable since 2005. Take up the interest expense by P102,187.50 in 2005 and P43,125.00 in 2006 or a total of P145,312.50 in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2006 No action taken. The Budget Officer still failed to appropriate the amount to pay the loans and the interest payable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that a supplemental budget be passed with a new
source of revenue or savings that could cover the legitimate and valid 2015
accounts payable that were already paid in 2016 and charged to a zero
unexpended balance in appropriation otherwise these expenses will be
disallowed in audit for being unauthorized.

We further recommend that Sections 84, 85 and 98 of P.D. 1445 be
strictly complied and that the local chief executive be held primarily
responsible for the execution and accountability for the annual and
supplemental budgets as provided in Section 416 of Republic Act 7180.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer be required to prepare an
“As Built Plan” and Program of Work to fully evaluate the construction of the
Project (Construction/Rehab of Dockside Boardwalk).

We further recommend that an engineering design and Program of
Work be prepared for every project to be undertaken by the Municipality. This
should be submitted to the Budget Office to be incorporated in the Annual
Procurement Plan which should be consistent with the approved annual
budget.

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor as the person primarily
responsible for the execution and accountability for the annual and
supplemental budgets be made to explain on the absence of an engineering
design and Program of Work in some projects in the General Fund thus
causing the expenses to be more than the budgeted amount otherwise the
difference can be considered as illegally disbursed and be refunded.

We recommend that management should submit an approved SB
Resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into contract for the
projects/programs to be implemented under the lump sum appropriation in
the Fund. It is the responsibility of the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the 20%
Development Fund be utilized for the priority development projects and
programs of the Municipality as mandated under DILG & DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We also recommend that all projects to be funded out of the 20%
Development Fund should partake the nature of investment or capital
expenditures.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
transfer the unexpended balance of the Calamity Fund amounting to
P5,157,797.61 to the trust fund since the purpose is solely for supporting
disaster risk reduction and management activities of the LDRRMC’s as
required under Section 6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We also recommend that management should strictly follow the
provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement and the Guidelines for the
implementation and an explanation or further investigation be made for the
deviation from the guidelines.

It is recommended that disbursements made by the municipality must
be supported with obligation request so as to enable the municipal budget
officer to certify the existence or the limit of its appropriation.

We require that the Municipal Government require the immediate
liquidation of the said cash advances through demand letters to be signed by the
Municipal Mayor or his representative.

We also recommend that in case of refusal by the employees to
liquidate or settle their accountabilities, direct the withholding of salaries to
be applied in satisfaction of the unsettled cash advance pursuant to Section
37 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 which is quoted hereunder, to wit:

Section 37 – Retention of Money for satisfaction of indebtedness to
Government “When any person is indebted to any government agency,
the Commission may direct the proper officer to withhold the payment
of any money due such person or his estate to be applied in
satisfaction of the indebtedness.”

Lastly, we recommend that clearances for retiring, resigning or
transferring officials be deferred until such time that they have liquidated
their outstanding cash advances.

We recommend that AAR management discontinue the practice of granting special cash advances to the concerned elected officials unless authorized by law or to any unauthorized official or employee and submit the documents of the cash advance granted for post audit.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances granted to elected officials amounted to P735,800.00 as follows: Elected P650,000.00 – P85,000.00 - travel expenses; submission of the cash advance documents of the cash advance granted for post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Disbursing Officer be allowed to draw cash advances equal to the net amount of the payrolls/list of payees only, specify clearly the pay check. period and attach said documents to the cash advance vouchers and to refund immediately any excess amount of the cash advance; We also recommend to require the Accountant to check the amount in the cash advance voucher against the net amount of the payrolls/lists and make corrections if there is any.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare and furnish the concerned auditor with a copy of the DILG-endorsed annual GPB identifying GAD programs or projects incorporating GAD concerns. Also, submit GAD AR within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year as required in item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Management submitted their Annual Budget for CY 2016 with GAD Plan attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board limit the utilization of the Special Education Fund to the programs and projects defined under RA 7160 and the DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 in order to maximize the benefits that the public school students and teachers could avail under this fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 SEF Guidelines was revised: The list of programs was updated and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 was used as a basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the discrepancies found in the general ledger transactions in 2012 be reconciled and adjusting/ reconciling items presented in the 2009 bank reconciliation statement be recorded in the books to be able to arrive at an adjusted cashbook balance reconciled with the bank balance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The monthly balances in the general ledger transactions in 2012 are already reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should exert effort to reconcile the accounts, provide subsidiary ledgers and to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the affected accounts to present accurate balances. Henceforth, consistency and accuracy in the recording of transactions should be observed.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2009 Adjusting entries were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the adjusting/reconciling items presented in the 2009 bank reconciliation statement be recorded in the books to be able to arrive at an adjusted cashbook balance reconciled with the bank balance. This would ensure the reliability and completeness of the cash in bank amount presented in the financial statements. We also recommend for an explanation from the accountant as to why the reconciling items have not been adjusted until this time.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The 2009 reconciling items were recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the officials and employees should refund the additional benefits they received since the grant thereof exceeded the 45% Personal Services limitation required in Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and LBM No. 54. Furthermore, any review comments by the Provincial Finance Committee on municipal budgets that needs to be complied with strictly must be taken into consideration before disbursing funds to avoid disallowances in post-audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2008 Management only give those benefits that are required by law.

observations.recommendation: Management should implement the program in line with the purpose of the PSF loan and in consonance with the provisions of the Memorandum of Agreement with the Office of the President for the benefit of majority of constituents. Issue specific guidelines on the granting and repayment of loans in line with the Agreement and see to it that borrowers fulfill the terms in the Affidavit of Undertaking. The working committee shall also undertake close monitoring of the livelihood projects, prepare progress reports and intensify collections from delinquent borrowers to reduce the risk of loss of the funds and to ensure the sustainability of the projects so that limited funds shall be maximized for the social and economic benefit of all concerned. The local chief executive should see to it that payment of the loan to the Office of the President is made as agreed upon in the MOA.
